name: SacHacks VII
description: This was my first hackathon and I still can't believe I - actually - won! Everything was new for me, working with people I didn't know until the hackathon, brainstorming ideas for our product, trying to find and creating THE design for our target audience, implementing the code for frontend with an actual client, getting data from a non-local environment and so more. What I learned from this hackathon: You have to be quick, especially at implementing the code:) I learned how to manage my time better when I have limited time for creating a website. I definitely improved my problem solving too, being in an environment that forces you to think and act fast lets you solve problems/bugs much faster too. (Extra note: Being able to see so many different projects with different visions in one place opened my eyes and allowed me to think more creatively as well.)

name: HackDavis 2026
description: This hackathon was my first big hackathon, with 400+ participants and 130+ projects. Me and my team built TransferBuddy AI, a web application that helps California community college students with their transfer process powered by AI. I worked on AI integraiton, AI API usage, backend - basically everything except the frontend. I learned how to use AI APIs, how to integrate them into a web application, how to manage backend and how to connect frontend-backend-AI.

name: HackStorm 2.0
description: HackStorm 2.0 was my first AIoT hackathon and the project that pushed me the furthest outside my comfort zone. Me and my teammate built SmartCane - an AI-powered smart cane that gives blind and visually impaired users active, always-on environmental awareness. The cane detects forward obstacles via ultrasonic sensors, overhead hazards and stairs via computer vision, traffic lights, elevator arrivals and can even describe the surrounding scene on demand using llama-4-scout model via Groq API. A fall detection system via an accelerometer triggers an SOS with GPS location. I worked on the AI and computer vision side, implementing features using Python, Microsoft Windows embedded TTS, llama-4-scout, and the OpenAI API. This project made me realize how impactful technology can be when it is built with a real human need in mind. This was my most challenging hackathon so far and I am glad I was able to work on a project that helps visually impaired people.
